title: (No AI) A married founder duo’s company, 14.ai, is replacing customer support teams at startups

creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
dc:creator: Ram Iyer
content: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.
contentSnippet: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

title: (No AI) How OpenAI caved to the Pentagon on AI surveillance
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Missiles flying through a computer screen.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/06/STKS515_CYBER_WARFARE_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman [announced](https://x.com/sama/status/2027578580159631610?s=20) that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with the Pentagon. The US government had just moved to blacklist Anthropic for [standing firm on two red lines](https://www.theverge.com/news/885773/anthropic-department-of-defense-dod-pentagon-refusal-terms-hegseth-dario-amodei) for military use: no mass surveillance of Americans and no lethal autonomous weapons (or AI systems with the power to kill targets [without human oversight](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/885963/anthropic-dod-pentagon-tech-workers-ai-labs-react)). Altman, however, implied that he'd found a unique way to keep those same limits in OpenAI's contract.

"Two of our most important safety principles are prohibitions on domestic mass surveillan …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/887309/openai-anthropic-dod-military-pentagon-contract-sam-altman-hegseth)
contentSnippet:
On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with the Pentagon. The US government had just moved to blacklist Anthropic for standing firm on two red lines for military use: no mass surveillance of Americans and no lethal autonomous weapons (or AI systems with the power to kill targets without human oversight). Altman, however, implied that he'd found a unique way to keep those same limits in OpenAI's contract.
"Two of our most important safety principles are prohibitions on domestic mass surveillan …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with the Pentagon. The US government had just moved to blacklist Anthropic for standing firm on two red lines for military use: no mass surveillance of […]

title: (No AI) A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
author: Andrew Liszewski
content:
![Two images of the Lenovo AI Workmate Concept with its robot arm folded and extended.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/02/lenovo1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The AI Workmate Concept can move and rotate to accomplish various tasks, but can it ever truly know love? | Image: Lenovo

Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts. Both are standalone desk devices designed to boost productivity while providing office workers with a bit of artificial dystopic companionship.

Lenovo describes its AI Workmate Concept as an "always-on desk companion," but it looks like a tiny robotic arm on a swiveling base with a bulbous screen on the end displaying an expressive pair of eyes. It doesn't look as engaging as a human coworker, but through local AI processing you can interact with the device as a smart assistant via vo …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/885228/lenovo-ai-workmate-companion-work-concept-robot-arm-desktop-clock-hub)

title: (No AI) Our agreement with the Department of War
content: Details on OpenAI’s contract with the Department of War, outlining safety red lines, legal protections, and how AI systems will be deployed in classified environments.
contentSnippet: Details on OpenAI’s contract with the Department of War, outlining safety red lines, legal protections, and how AI systems will be deployed in classified environments.

creator: Joel Khalili
title: (No AI) The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
dc:creator: Joel Khalili
content: As AI labs gorge themselves on compute, data center operators have headed north in search of cheap and plentiful energy.
contentSnippet: As AI labs gorge themselves on compute, data center operators have headed north in search of cheap and plentiful energy.

creator: Maxwell Zeff, Will Knight, Lauren Goode, Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) Anthropic Hits Back After US Military Labels It a ‘Supply Chain Risk’
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff, Will Knight, Lauren Goode, Paresh Dave
content: Anthropic says it would be “legally unsound” for the Pentagon to blacklist its technology after talks over military use of its artificial intelligence models broke down.
contentSnippet: Anthropic says it would be “legally unsound” for the Pentagon to blacklist its technology after talks over military use of its artificial intelligence models broke down.
